15.anchor: share your love only with your wife.

16.note: Hebrew Why spill your springs in the streets, / your streams in the city squares?

2.anchor: Guard my instructions as you guard your own eyes.
2.note: Hebrew as the pupil of your eye.

20.anchor: and won’t return until later this month.
20.note: Hebrew until the moon is full.

22.anchor: He was like a stag caught in a trap,
22.note: As in Greek and Syriac versions; Hebrew reads slaughter, as shackles are for the discipline of a fool.

27.anchor: Her house is the road to the grave.

18.anchor: it is the road to the grave.
18.note: Hebrew to the spirits of the dead.

18.anchor: Her guests are in the depths of the grave.

12.anchor: Let’s swallow them alive, like the grave

30.note: Or and those who win souls are wise.

31.anchor: what will happen to wicked sinners?
31.note: Greek version reads If the righteous are barely saved, / what will happen to godless sinners?

26.anchor: For a prostitute will bring you to poverty,
26.note: Hebrew to a loaf of bread.

12.anchor: just as a father corrects a child in whom he delights.
12.note: Greek version reads loves, / and he punishes those he accepts as his children.

34.anchor: but is gracious to the humble.

1.anchor: A wise child accepts a parent’s discipline;
1.note: Hebrew A wise son accepts his father’s discipline.

15.anchor: a treacherous person is headed for destruction.
15.note: As in Greek version; Hebrew reads the way of the treacherous is lasting.

33.note: As in Greek and Syriac versions; Hebrew lacks not.

10.anchor: but a bold reproof promotes peace.
10.note: As in Greek version; Hebrew reads but babbling fools fall flat on their faces.

30.note: The meaning of the Hebrew is uncertain.

6.anchor: is a vanishing mist and a deadly trap.
6.note: As in Greek version; Hebrew reads mist for those who seek death.

8.anchor: and their reign of terror will come to an end.
8.note: The Greek version includes an additional proverb: God blesses a man who gives cheerfully, / but his worthless deeds will come to an end.

26.anchor: The godly give good advice to their friends;
26.note: Or The godly are cautious in friendship; The godly are freed from evil.

5.anchor: The wise are mightier than the strong,
5.note: As in Greek version; Hebrew reads A wise man is strength.

23.note: As in Greek version; Hebrew reads Burning.

25.anchor: Their hearts are full of many evils.

13.anchor: Get a deposit if he does it for foreigners.
13.note: As in Greek and Latin versions (see also 20:16); Hebrew reads for a promiscuous woman.

7.anchor: They are always thinking about how much it costs.
7.note: The meaning of the Hebrew is uncertain.

14.anchor: may well save them from death.

24.anchor: What a pleasure to have children who are wise.
24.note: Hebrew to have a wise son.

1.anchor: The sayings of Agur son of Jakeh contain this message.
1.note: Or son of Jakeh from Massa; son of Jakeh, an oracle.

1.anchor: I am weary and worn out, O God.
1.note: The Hebrew can also be translated The man declares this to Ithiel, / to Ithiel and to Ucal.

15.note: Hebrew two daughters who cry out, “Give, give!”

10.anchor: but the upright seek to help them.
10.note: Or The bloodthirsty hate blameless people, / and they seek to kill the upright; The bloodthirsty hate blameless people; / as for the upright, they seek their life.

20.anchor: or pouring vinegar in a wound.
20.note: As in Greek version; Hebrew reads pouring vinegar on soda.

1.anchor: The sayings of King Lemuel contain this message,
1.note: Or of Lemuel, king of Massa; of King Lemuel, an oracle.
